_README: ILLUSTRATIVE SAMPLE CORPUS — NOT the Tasmanian Planning Scheme. Clause IDs follow the real SPP/LPS structure but all text is paraphrased and provenance is 'SAMPLE'. Replace via ingest/scheme.py once outbound network access to tpso.planning.tas.gov.au is enabled.

chunks.title: Categories of use or development
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): A use is classified against the applicable zone Use Table as No Permit Required, Permitted, Discretionary, or Prohibited. Where a use is not listed and cannot be reasonably attributed to a listed class, it is Prohibited.

chunks.title: Use class: Residential
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): 'Residential' means use of land for self-contained or shared living accommodation. Examples include a single dwelling, multiple dwellings, and a home-based business that is ancillary to a dwelling.

chunks.clause_id: SPP Use Class Definitions — Visitor Accommodation
chunks.title: Use class: Visitor accommodation
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): 'Visitor accommodation' means use of land for temporary or short-term accommodation of persons away from their normal place of residence. Examples include a hotel, motel, bed and breakfast, and short-stay letting of a dwelling.

chunks.title: General Residential Zone — Zone Purpose
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): To provide for residential use and development at suburban densities; to provide for compatible non-residential uses that primarily serve the local community; and to ensure development is consistent with the established or preferred residential character of the area.

chunks.title: General Residential Zone — Use Table
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased) Use Table entries: Residential (single dwelling) — No Permit Required if standards met, otherwise Permitted. Residential (multiple dwellings) — Permitted. Visitor accommodation — Discretionary. General retail and hire — Discretionary, qualified by floor-area limits. General industrial — Prohibited.

chunks.title: Building height — Acceptable Solution A1
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): A1 — Building height must be not more than 8.5 m.
chunks.performance_criterion: SAMPLE (paraphrased): P1 — Building height must satisfy the amenity of adjoining lots having regard to overshadowing, visual scale, and streetscape; merit assessment requires shadow diagrams and a streetscape analysis.

chunks.title: Setbacks and building envelope — Acceptable Solution A1
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): A1 — A dwelling must have a frontage setback not less than 4.5 m, and side and rear setbacks in accordance with the building envelope projected at 45 degrees from 3 m above boundaries.
chunks.performance_criterion: SAMPLE (paraphrased): P1 — Setbacks must provide reasonable separation, sunlight access and privacy for adjoining lots having regard to topography and existing development pattern.

chunks.title: Rural Living Zone — Zone Purpose
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): To provide for residential use on larger lots in a rural setting, where services are limited, and to ensure use and development does not compromise primary industry on adjoining land.

chunks.title: Bushfire-Prone Areas Code — Application
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): This code applies to use or development on land within a bushfire-prone area, including any vulnerable use or subdivision. A bushfire hazard management plan certified by an accredited person is generally required where the acceptable solution thresholds are not met.

chunks.title: Bushfire — hazard management areas — Acceptable Solution A1
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): A1 — Insufficient defendable space / hazard management area provided; an acceptable solution is met only where a certified bushfire hazard management plan demonstrates compliant separation, access and water supply.
chunks.performance_criterion: SAMPLE (paraphrased): P1 — Where A1 is not met, a TFS/accredited-person bushfire hazard management plan must demonstrate the tolerable bushfire risk is achieved for the use.

chunks.title: Natural Assets Code — Waterway and riparian protection
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): This code applies to development within a waterway, wetland, riparian or priority vegetation area. Building and works within a defined riparian setback are Discretionary and require assessment of impacts on bank stability, water quality and habitat.

chunks.title: Scenic Protection Code — Application
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Applies to land within a scenic protection area or scenic road corridor shown on an overlay. Development must be sited and designed to minimise visual prominence, using colour, scale and vegetation retention.

chunks.title: Coastal Erosion Hazard Code — Application
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Applies to land within a coastal erosion hazard band. Development must be set back from the hazard, or demonstrate via a coastal hazard report that the tolerable risk is met for the design life of the development.

chunks.title: Hobart LPS — General Residential height variation (Specific Area Plan)
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Within the Hobart LPS Skyline / Scenic Specific Area Plan, building height in the General Residential Zone is reduced to 7.2 m to protect views of kunanyi / Mount Wellington from designated vantage points.
chunks.performance_criterion: SAMPLE (paraphrased): P1 — Additional height may be considered where a visual impact assessment shows no material loss of identified views.

chunks.zone_or_code: C11.0 Coastal Inundation Hazard Code (GSB overlay)
chunks.title: Glamorgan-Spring Bay LPS — Coastal Inundation overlay
chunks.text: SAMPLE (paraphrased): Land along the GSB foreshore is mapped within a medium coastal inundation hazard band; habitable floor levels must be set above the defined flood level or a coastal hazard report must justify a lower level.
